title: Palaruvi Falls
tagline: Palaruvi Falls — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Palaruvi Falls is a waterfall located in Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Palaruvi Falls is a waterfall located in Kollam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is the 32nd highest waterfall in India. Palaruvi (literally "stream of milk") falls from a height of 300 feet (91 m) and is situated at Aryankavu in the Kollam district of the south Indian state of Kerala.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Aryankavu Sastha Temple
topPlaces.description: The Aryankavu Sastha Temple is a Hindu temple located in Aryankavu in the Kollam district of the Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the five major temples dedicated to Shasta in Kerala, the others being Kulathupuzha, Achankovil, Sabarimala and Kanthamala.

topPlaces.name: Aryankavu
topPlaces.description: Aryankavu is a village located in the Kollam district of the Indian state of Kerala. It lies close to the border between Kerala and Tamil Nadu near Thenmala.

topPlaces.name: Pudur, Tirunelveli
topPlaces.description: Pudur (S) is a panchayat town in Tenkasi district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Pathimoonnu Kannara Bridge
topPlaces.description: The Pathimoonnu Kannara Bridge, also called the "13 Arch Bridge", is a historic British-era structure on the Kollam-Sengottai railway line in India. It is situated at Kazhuthurutty in the Kollam district of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Ambanad Hills
topPlaces.description: Ambanad Hills or Ambanad is a hill station in Punalur Taluk in the eastern part of Kollam district in Kerala, India. This is one among the few tea and orange plantation areas in Kollam district.

topPlaces.name: Sengottai
topPlaces.description: Sengottai (also known as Shencottah, Chenkotta or Shenkottai) is a Town in the Tenkasi district, of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Aintharuvi
topPlaces.description: Aintharuvi ( five falls ) is a group of five waterfalls, that is near the Coutrallam Falls of Tamil Nadu, in India. It is located in Tenkasi.

topPlaces.name: Aintharuviar River
topPlaces.description: The Aintharuviar River is a river flowing in the Tirunelveli district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a tributary of the Chittar River, which itself is a major tributary of the Thamirabarani River.

items.note: The Aryankavu Sastha Temple is a Hindu temple located in Aryankavu in the Kollam district of the Indian state of Kerala. It is one of the five major temples dedicated to Shasta in Kerala, the others being Kulathupuzha, Achankovil, Sabarimala and Kanthamala.
Aryankavu is a village located in the Kollam district of the Indian state of Kerala. It lies close to the border between Kerala and Tamil Nadu near Thenmala.
Pudur (S) is a panchayat town in Tenkasi district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

The Pathimoonnu Kannara Bridge, also called the "13 Arch Bridge", is a historic British-era structure on the Kollam-Sengottai railway line in India. It is situated at Kazhuthurutty in the Kollam district of Kerala.
Ambanad Hills or Ambanad is a hill station in Punalur Taluk in the eastern part of Kollam district in Kerala, India. This is one among the few tea and orange plantation areas in Kollam district.
Sengottai (also known as Shencottah, Chenkotta or Shenkottai) is a Town in the Tenkasi district, of Tamil Nadu, India.
Aintharuvi ( five falls ) is a group of five waterfalls, that is near the Coutrallam Falls of Tamil Nadu, in India. It is located in Tenkasi.

The Aintharuviar River is a river flowing in the Tirunelveli district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a tributary of the Chittar River, which itself is a major tributary of the Thamirabarani River.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Palaruvi Falls?
faq.a: The best time to visit Palaruvi Falls is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Palaruvi Falls?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (62 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (59 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Palaruvi Falls?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Palaruvi Falls at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Palaruvi Falls?
faq.a: Stays in Palaruvi Falls start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Palaruvi Falls?
faq.a: The top attractions are Aryankavu Sastha Temple, Aryankavu, Pudur, Tirunelveli, Pathimoonnu Kannara Bridge, Ambanad Hills.
